_comment_entity_detail_raw_instrumentati: Default ON (#484/#485). Measures TRUE pre-compaction raw_tokens for the entity_detail phase by serialising the full uncompacted prior entity state alongside the compacted prompt actually sent (measurement-only; the prompt sent to the model and the extraction output are unchanged). Set false to skip the extra per-entity json.dumps on long sessions with large catalog entries, where it can become a per-turn CPU/memory bottleneck; when off, entity_detail raw_tokens falls back to the compressed size (pre-#484 behaviour) and compression_ratio returns to 1.0 for that phase.

context_optimizations._comment: L1+L4 type-tiered PC relationship cap (epic #477). Default ON: caps PC entity-detail relationships by type: permanent bonds kept uncapped, volatile tail trimmed to pc_rel_volatile_tail_cap. Also promotes permanent-bond types in the relationship-mapper budget trim. Set false to restore pre-#477 behaviour (byte-identical to the pre-#477 baseline -- i.e. main before #477 -- when the flag is absent or false; note that once this default-ON change merges, main itself ships the flag ON); a same-commit flag-OFF run serves as the A/B control.
context_optimizations._comment_relevance_shadow_logging: S0 relevance-scoped entity_detail shadow (epic #477, lever (b)/A2a, #494). Default OFF and MEASUREMENT-ONLY: when on, each turn's extraction log gets an extra `relevance_detail_shadow` record comparing the actual cap-6 detail set against the set the existing relevance signal (_select_context_aware_entities, #233) WOULD pick -- including the cap-dropped-but-relevant count and the critical safety metric referenced_but_would_drop_count (entities referenced this turn that relevance would drop; must be ~0 for the signal to be sound). It NEVER changes which entities are detailed, the prompts, the LLM calls, or any extracted output: flag-OFF and flag-ON produce byte-identical extraction results, only the extra log stream differs. This S0 shadow validates the relevance signal before the S1 (A2a) behaviour-changing slice gates DetailSet on it.
context_optimizations._comment_raw_io_capture: RAW-IO capture instrumentation (epic #477, step 1). Default OFF and MEASUREMENT-ONLY: when on, each LLM call in the extraction pipeline (entity_detail, discovery, relationship_mapper, event) tees one JSONL record to framework/raw-io-capture.jsonl containing the VERBATIM prompt (system+user), the VERBATIM un-parsed completion, the phase, turn id, entity id (for per-entity phases like entity_detail/PC), and per-call input/output token counts (output = decode tokens, the never-before-logged 87%-wall-clock driver; taken from the backend usage.completion_tokens when present, else a char-heuristic estimate flagged *_tokens_estimated=true). It NEVER changes the prompts, the LLM calls, the parsing, or any extracted output: flag-OFF and flag-ON produce byte-identical extraction results and flag-OFF writes NO artifact and does zero capture work. Its purpose is to VALIDATE on real model output (not a parsed-catalog proxy) what entity_detail actually emits, and to expose per-entity PC-call-isolated input tokens. Strict bool parse (is true). Exercise it with one flag-on extraction run to produce the data; it needs no token A/B to merge.
context_optimizations._comment_relationship_demotion_shadow: Relationship-demotion shadow logging (epic #477, #495 step 1). Default OFF and MEASUREMENT-ONLY: when on, each relationship serialized at BOTH relationship-context seams -- A = entity_detail prior-context (the PC relationship block is the #495 driver), B = relationship_mapper existing-rels -- emits one record to framework/relationship-demotion-shadow.jsonl with turn, owner, target_id, type, status, staleness (current_turn - last_updated_turn), core_degree (endpoint connectivity from the bidirectional relationship-index), mention_source (real_mention = the rel's endpoint was genuinely named in the turn text vs injected_floor = a char-player relationship kept only because it sits on the PC floor vs none), would_demote, and seam. It NEVER changes the prompts, LLM calls, tiering, caps, or any extracted output: flag-OFF writes NO artifact and does zero work (byte-identical to baseline), flag-ON is byte-identical extraction with only the extra log stream. The would_demote policy REUSES the existing discrete tier-4-OMIT scoring (_score_relationship_tier) -- it is a documented measurement assumption, NOT shipped behaviour and NOT a new tuned scalar threshold (Rule 10). This is Step 1 of #495 (the relationship-context lever); it does NOT change demotion behaviour. Strict bool parse (is true). Exercise it with one flag-on extraction run to produce the telemetry.
context_optimizations._comment_relationship_mapper_delta_outpu: Delta-output relationship_mapper lever (epic #477, #495). Default OFF, strict bool parse (is true). When on, the relationship-mapper uses templates/extraction/relationship-mapper-delta.md instead of relationship-mapper.md, instructing the model to emit ONLY new or materially-changed relationships (NEW (source_id,target_id) pair, materially-changed current_relationship, changed type, or changed direction). Confidence-only drift is explicitly NOT a change. The safety contract lives in the template: the system already keeps every existing relationship automatically -- OMITTING one does NOT delete it -- and merge semantics are unchanged (additive/update-only), so an omitted unchanged relationship stays as-is. Selected via the single helper _relationship_mapper_template_name at all four relationship-mapper call sites (parallel extractor, prompt-metrics, serial, retry). Flag-OFF is byte-identical to baseline (template name relationship-mapper). NOTE: when ON, last_updated_turn means 'last MATERIAL change' rather than 'last seen', so downstream artifacts keyed on re-emission recency (relationship-index.json, scene-graph.json, synthesis/wiki timelines, in-run recency tiering) intentionally diverge.
context_optimizations._comment_relationship_mapper_drop_histor: Drop-history input lever (epic #477, #495). Default OFF, strict bool parse (is true). When on, the `history` key is stripped from the relationship-mapper INPUT block (the existing-relationships JSON sent to the model), applied to read-only dict copies in _format_rel_by_tier across every tier -- the catalog's entity['relationships'] is NEVER mutated. Flag-OFF is byte-identical to baseline (history retained). Independent of relationship_mapper_delta_output; either may be enabled alone.

batch_entity_detail._comment: L2 batched entity_detail (epic #477, #491). Default OFF: when on, the entity-detail system template + turn text are sent ONCE per batch instead of once per entity, detailing multiple low-salience entities per shared-context call with hard-delimited per-entity blocks. PC (char-player), brand-new entities, and entities with confidence >= high_confidence_threshold stay SOLO to avoid cross-entity attribute bleed; only the lower-salience tail is batched in groups of batch_size. Parse failure or any missing/invalid entity falls back to a solo call (no dropped entities). Flag-OFF is byte-identical to the per-entity baseline (the A/B control).

relevance_scoped_detail._comment: S1 relevance-scoped entity_detail selection (epic #477, lever (b)/A2a, #498; builds on the S0 shadow #494). Default OFF and byte-identical to the cap-6 baseline when off (the A/B control). When on in mode 'recover_only' (S1a), the detail set becomes the cap-6 set UNION the relevance recoveries -- cap-dropped entities the existing #233 relevance signal (_select_context_aware_entities) marks relevant (PC, new, or mentioned/co-located/one-hop-active) -- so the ~245 real updates the cap-6 silently drops are recovered. Recover-only is a SUPERSET of the cap-6 set: it ADDS recoveries and drops NOTHING currently detailed. max_detail_entities is the ceiling (telemetry-derived from S0 would_select p95 = 10, max = 18; caps the climax spike, binds on < 5% of turns); on bind it trims the lowest-relevance recovery tail, never the cap-6 set and never the floor (PC/new/mentioned). It is clamped >= the hard cap (_MAX_DETAIL_ENTITIES_PER_TURN = 6). The 'mode' field is the forward-compat seam for S1b ('drop_filler', not implemented here); an unrecognised mode normalises to 'recover_only'. S1 is gated on a QUALITY update-retention A/B vs a cap-6 control, NOT the 60h token A/B (its token delta is ~flat by design).
